seoTitle: Kingston vs Lawson — Climate Risk Comparison | ClimateNest
seoDescription: Compare climate risk scores between Kingston and Lawson. See which suburb is safer from flood, bushfire, heat, and storm hazards.
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Kingston and Lawson.
heading: Kingston vs Lawson — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Kingston, Australian Capital Territory and Lawson, Australian Capital Territory. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Lawson has a lower overall climate risk score (37/100) compared to Kingston (42/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Kingston or Lawson safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Kingston has a flood risk score of 47/100, while Lawson scores 53/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Kingston scores 36/100 for bushfire risk. Lawson scores 37/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Kingston or Lawson?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Lawson has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 1.75% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
